pattern: Each probe against the picked number returns a strict below/at/above signal, so the candidate range can only ever be cut in half no matter which side the target lands on.
hints: Trying every number from 1 up costs up to n calls; the response never contradicts itself between probes.
Keep a lo/hi bracket around the picked number and query its midpoint each round.
A high response drops hi below the midpoint, a low response raises lo above it, and an exact match ends the search — compute the midpoint as lo + (hi - lo) // 2 to avoid overflow on very large n.

edge_inputs.description: single candidate, which is the pick
pick is the very first candidate
pick is the very last candidate
pick sits in the middle of a small range
near the 32-bit ceiling with the lowest possible pick — overflow trap for a naive (lo+hi) midpoint
near the 32-bit ceiling with the highest possible pick
two candidates, pick is the lower one
two candidates, pick is the higher one

stress.description: random n with a random pick inside it
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 2147483647)))
    pick = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [n, pick]
